title: pendingPermissionResult bridge in MainActivity: defend this
description: MainActivity stores MethodChannel.Result in pendingPermissionResult, then completes it from onRequestPermissionsResult. This correctly bridges the async Android permission dialog back to a synchronous Dart Future. A future contributor might 'simplify' this to fire-and-forget (call requestPermissions, return result.success(true) immediately). Defend: the sequencing matters — startService should only be called after permission state is resolved to avoid the OS killing startForeground() before POST_NOTIFICATIONS is granted on API 33+.
close_reason: Decision records — informational only, no code change needed
